div.p: SEQ_LT(a, b) logic for TCP Window Management and Wraparound Handling

ol.li: Integer Promotion: C standards dictate that subtraction of unsigned types wraps modulo 2ⁿ.
Signed Conversion: Casting the result to int32_t maps the upper half of the unsigned range [2³¹, 2³²-1] to negative values [-2³¹, -1].

Window Constraints: This logic is the mathematical foundation for PAWS (Protection Against Wrapped Sequence numbers). It requires that the sequence number cannot wrap more than once within the Maximum Segment Lifetime (MSL).

div.p: a < b is true if and only if:
This effectively defines "less than" as being in the preceding half of the circular sequence space.

g.text: a is before or equal b
a is after or equal b
Warning: These macros fail if |a - b| ≥ 2³¹

div.p: The Problem: Naive comparison treats the space as linear. TCP sees 0xFFFFFFF0 as being "just before" 0x10. The subtraction method correctly calculates the signed distance across the wraparound boundary.
The result 0xFFFFFFE0 in a 32-bit signed integer register is exactly -32.
Since -32 < 0, the comparison SEQ_LT succeeds, correctly handling the wrap.
